content_en: Despite the Damascenes' fears of his political ambitions, they developed friendly relations with him that were better than before these events took place.439 They supported his political position in northern Syria more strongly, and the Damascenes implicitly acknowledged the strength of his political influence, and asked him to help them against the Kingdom of Jerusalem, their former ally.440 The weakness of the rulers of Damascus: this second campaign of the Crusaders highlighted the degree of weakness that the atabegs of Damascus had reached, as they were unable to confront the Crusader advance towards them and were forced to ask for outside military help. No doubt this weakness was noticed by Noor ad-Deen Mahmood, and that deepened his resolve to unite the Islamic front by incorporating Damascus.441 The destruction of the fortress of al-'Uraymah: Noor ad-Deen Mahmood took advantage of the first opportunity he had to work with Mu'een ad-Deen Unur. Raymond, Count of Tripoli, had sought help from Noor ad-Deen against one of the Frankish princes who had joined the Second Crusade with the French army, Bertrand Count of Toulouse. Bertrand did not go back with the Franks after the campaign ended, but rather headed north by sea, parallel with the coast, until he drew level with the County of Tripoli, whereupon he came ashore with his knights and breached the fortress of al- 'Uraymah, which belonged to Tripoli, and fortified himself therein, announcing his intention to capture Tripoli as he regarded himself as more entitled to it than was Raymond. Raymond was unable to defeat him, so he tried to seek help from the other Frankish states. When he got no response from them, he sent word seeking help from Noor ad-Deen and Mu'een adDeen, who hastened to besiege the fortress with their troops. They captured it, took everyone in it into captivity, and destroyed the fortress, levelling it to the ground; each then went back to his city.442 This incident indicates the extent of the bad effect that the failure of the Second Crusade had on the situation of the Frankish states in the Islamic East.443 The Muslims lost their fear of the Crusaders. A number of historians regard the failure of the Second Crusade as a turning point in the history of the Muslim-Christian conflict. In Syria it led to a decline in fear of the Crusaders, which encouraged Muslim forces to launch audacious raids on the Crusader states. It also provided the opportunity for another star of Jihad against the Crusaders to emerge, namely Noor ad-Deen Mahmood Zangi, who revived his father's plan to unite the Islamic front against the Crusaders, which is the plan that would be completed by Salah ad-Deen and was to pave the way for the end of the Crusades. Noor ad-Deen succeeded in taking advantage of circumstances following the failure of the Second Crusade to unite Syria under his leadership this time, at the expense of the Emir of Damascus. He then resumed his Jihad against the Crusaders with a success which encouraged other Muslim forces such as the Seljuks of Rum, the Artuqids and the Turkmens to confront the Crusaders, especially in Edessa and Antioch, and also to form alliances. In this way Noor ad-Deen Zangi was able to unite all of Syria under his leadership, from Edessa in the north to Harran in the south, and to establish a united Islamic state centred on Damascus. This was the first step towards forming the front that stretched from the Euphrates to the Nile, to truly confront the Crusade. CHAPTER IV Wisdom of Noor ad-Deen's Dealings with the Fatimid State Roots of the Ismaili Shiites and the Fatimid state O'fter the death of Imam Ja'far ibn Muhammad as-Sadiq, the Shiites divided into two groups who both attributed themselves to Ja'far as-Sadiq. One group transferred the imamate to his son Moosa al-Kazim; they are the 'Ithna 'Ashari' Shiites. The other group denied that he was the Imam and said that the Imam after Ja'far was his son Isma'eel (or Ismail); this group is known as the Ismaili Shiites.445 'Abdul-Qahir al-Baghdadi said concerning the Ismailis, "They transferred the imamate to Ja'far and claimed that the Imam after him was his son Isma'eel."446 Al-Shahrastani commented: The Ismailis differ from the Moosawis (Musavis) and Ithna 'Asharis in that they attribute the imamate to Isma'eel ibn Ja'far, his oldest son who was mentioned in the texts initially. They said that as-Sadiq (may Allah have mercy on him) did not marry any woman after his mother — that is, the mother of Isma'eel — and he did not take any slave woman as his concubine, as was the way of the Messenger of Allah (SAAW) with Khadeejah and as was the way of 'Ali with Fatimah.447 The Ismailis are one of the branches of the Shiites and are named after Isma'eel ibn Ja'far as-Sadiq, but they are also known by names other than Ismailis, such as al-Batiniyah. They were given this name because of their belief that for every outward, visible manifestation (dhahir) there is an inward, invisible manifestation (batin), and for every revelation there is an interpretation. Among them are the Qaramitah and Mazdakiyah, by which two names they were known in Iraq. In Khorasan they were called atTa'leemiyah al-Mulhidah (Atheist Educationalists).
content_id:
Meskipun orang-orang Damaskus khawatir akan ambisi politiknya, mereka mengembangkan hubungan yang ramah dengannya yang lebih baik dari sebelum peristiwa-peristiwa ini terjadi. Mereka mendukung posisi politiknya di Suriah utara secara lebih kuat, dan orang-orang Damaskus secara implisit mengakui kekuatan pengaruh politiknya, serta memintanya untuk membantu mereka melawan Kerajaan Yerusalem, sekutu mereka sebelumnya.

Kelemahan para penguasa Damaskus — Kampanye Tentara Salib kedua ini menyoroti tingkat kelemahan yang telah dicapai oleh para atabeg Damaskus, karena mereka tidak mampu menghadapi kemajuan Tentara Salib menuju mereka dan terpaksa meminta bantuan militer dari luar. Tidak diragukan lagi kelemahan ini diperhatikan oleh Noor ad-Deen Mahmood, dan hal itu semakin memperdalam tekadnya untuk menyatukan front Islam dengan memasukkan Damaskus.

Penghancuran benteng al-Uraymah — Noor ad-Deen Mahmood memanfaatkan kesempatan pertama yang ia miliki untuk bekerja sama dengan Mu'een ad-Deen Unur. Raymond, Hitungan Tripoli, telah meminta bantuan dari Noor ad-Deen melawan salah seorang pangeran Frank yang bergabung dalam Perang Salib Kedua bersama pasukan Prancis, yaitu Bertrand Hitungan Toulouse. Bertrand tidak kembali bersama pasukan Frank setelah kampanye berakhir, melainkan bergerak ke utara melalui laut, sejajar dengan pantai, hingga ia sejajar dengan Kabupaten Tripoli, kemudian ia mendarat bersama para ksatrianya dan menyerbu benteng al-Uraymah, yang termasuk milik Tripoli, dan membentengi dirinya di dalamnya, mengumumkan niatnya untuk merebut Tripoli karena ia menganggap dirinya lebih berhak atasnya daripada Raymond.

Raymond tidak mampu mengalahkannya, sehingga ia mencoba mencari bantuan dari negara-negara Frank lainnya. Ketika ia tidak mendapat respons dari mereka, ia mengirim pesan meminta bantuan kepada Noor ad-Deen dan Mu'een ad-Deen, yang bergegas mengepung benteng itu bersama pasukan mereka. Mereka merebutnya, menawan semua orang di dalamnya, dan menghancurkan benteng itu hingga rata dengan tanah; masing-masing kemudian kembali ke kotanya. Insiden ini mengindikasikan sejauh mana dampak buruk yang ditimbulkan oleh kegagalan Perang Salib Kedua terhadap situasi negara-negara Frank di Timur Islam.

Kaum Muslim kehilangan rasa takut mereka terhadap Tentara Salib — Sejumlah sejarawan menganggap kegagalan Perang Salib Kedua sebagai titik balik dalam sejarah konflik Muslim-Kristen. Di Suriah, hal itu mengakibatkan berkurangnya rasa takut terhadap Tentara Salib, yang mendorong pasukan Muslim untuk melancarkan serangan-serangan berani terhadap negara-negara Tentara Salib. Hal itu juga memberikan kesempatan bagi bintang Jihad lainnya melawan Tentara Salib untuk muncul, yaitu Noor ad-Deen Mahmood Zangi, yang menghidupkan kembali rencana ayahnya untuk menyatukan front Islam melawan Tentara Salib, yang merupakan rencana yang akan diselesaikan oleh Salahuddin dan membuka jalan bagi berakhirnya Perang Salib.

Noor ad-Deen berhasil memanfaatkan keadaan setelah kegagalan Perang Salib Kedua untuk menyatukan Suriah di bawah kepemimpinannya kali ini, dengan mengorbankan Emir Damaskus. Ia kemudian melanjutkan Jihadnya melawan Tentara Salib dengan keberhasilan yang mendorong kekuatan-kekuatan Muslim lainnya seperti Seljuk Rum, Artuqid, dan Turkmen untuk menghadapi Tentara Salib, dan juga untuk membentuk aliansi. Dengan cara ini Noor ad-Deen Zangi mampu menyatukan seluruh Suriah di bawah kepemimpinannya, dari Edessa di utara hingga Harran di selatan, dan mendirikan sebuah negara Islam bersatu yang berpusat di Damaskus. Inilah langkah pertama menuju pembentukan front yang membentang dari Eufrat hingga Nil, untuk benar-benar menghadapi Perang Salib.

BAB IV — Kebijaksanaan Noor ad-Deen dalam Berhubungan dengan Negara Fatimiyah — Akar-akar Syiah Ismaili dan negara Fatimiyah: Setelah wafatnya Imam Ja'far ibn Muhammad as-Sadiq, kaum Syiah terbagi menjadi dua kelompok yang keduanya menisbatkan diri mereka kepada Ja'far as-Sadiq. Satu kelompok mengalihkan imamah kepada putranya Musa al-Kazim; mereka adalah kaum Syiah Itsna Asyari. Kelompok lainnya mengingkari bahwa ia adalah Imam dan mengatakan bahwa Imam setelah Ja'far adalah putranya Ismail; kelompok ini dikenal sebagai kaum Syiah Ismaili. Abdul-Qahir al-Baghdadi berkata mengenai kaum Ismaili, "Mereka mengalihkan imamah kepada Ja'far dan mengklaim bahwa Imam setelahnya adalah putranya Ismail." Al-Syahrastani mengomentari: Kaum Ismaili berbeda dari kaum Musawi dan Itsna Asyari dalam hal bahwa mereka menisbatkan imamah kepada Ismail ibn Ja'far, putra sulungnya yang awalnya disebutkan dalam teks-teks. Kaum Ismaili adalah salah satu cabang dari Syiah dan dinamai menurut Ismail ibn Ja'far as-Sadiq, namun mereka juga dikenal dengan nama-nama selain Ismaili, seperti al-Batiniyah. Mereka diberi nama ini karena keyakinan mereka bahwa untuk setiap manifestasi lahiriah yang tampak (dhahir) terdapat manifestasi batiniah yang tersembunyi (batin), dan untuk setiap wahyu terdapat tafsir. Di antara mereka terdapat Qaramitah dan Mazdakiyah, dua nama yang dengannya mereka dikenal di Irak. Di Khorasan mereka disebut at-Ta'limiyah al-Mulhidah (Pendidik Ateis).
